formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Operating Profit Ratio",
    "formula": "Operating Profit Ratio = (Operating Profit / Total Revenue from Operations) × 100",
    "variables": "Operating Profit, Total Revenue from Operations",
    "units": "%",
    "notes": "To calculate the operating profit ratio of a company."
  },
  {
    "name": "Gross Profit Ratio",
    "formula": "Gross Profit Ratio = (Gross Profit / Total Revenue from Operations) × 100",
    "variables": "Gross Profit, Total Revenue from Operations",
    "units": "%",
    "notes": "To calculate the gross profit ratio of a company."
  },
  {
    "name": "Return on Capital Employed (ROCE)",
    "formula": "ROCE = (Profit Before Interest and Tax / Capital Employed) × 100",
    "variables": "Profit Before Interest and Tax, Capital Employed",
    "units": "%",
    "notes": "To calculate the return on capital employed of a company."
  },
  {
    "name": "Debt-Equity Ratio",
    "formula": "Debt-Equity Ratio = Total Debt / Total Share Capital",
    "variables": "Total Debt, Total Share Capital",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "To calculate the debt-equity ratio of a company."
  },
  {
    "name": "Interest Coverage Ratio",
    "formula": "Interest Coverage Ratio = Profit Before Interest and Tax / Interest Expenses",
    "variables": "Profit Before Interest and Tax, Interest Expenses",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "To calculate the interest coverage ratio of a company."
  },
  {
    "name": "Current Ratio",
    "formula": "Current Ratio = Current Assets / Current Liabilities",
    "variables": "Current Assets, Current Liabilities",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "To calculate the current ratio of a company."
  },
  {
    "name": "Acid Test Ratio",
    "formula": "Acid Test Ratio = (Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities",
    "variables": "Current Assets, Inventory, Current Liabilities",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "To calculate the acid test ratio of a company."
  }
]
```

Note: The formulas provided are based on the Analysis of Financial Statements chapter in the CBSE Class 12 Accountancy syllabus. They are crucial for calculating various ratios that help in analyzing the financial performance of a company.
